text: Teams move faster when they own the problem, not just the solution.
Activity is visible. Value is what matters.
A roadmap should create alignment, not certainty.
The quality of a decision depends more on information than hierarchy.
Good leaders create autonomy. Great leaders create more leaders.
The longer information travels, the slower an organisation becomes.
Technology rarely limits a company. People and incentives do.
Customers don't care about features. They care about outcomes.
The purpose of planning is understanding, not prediction.
Most complexity is created one exception at a time.
Trust scales better than control.
A team becomes stronger when knowledge is shared and ownership is distributed.
Experience is useful when it increases curiosity, not certainty.
The best process is the one people no longer need to think about.
Communication succeeds when understanding happens.
Most organisational problems appear as technology problems first.
A backlog is not a strategy.
Learning compounds faster than planning.
The goal is not to deliver software. The goal is to solve a problem.
Empowered people need less management and create more value.
People can appoint managers. Teams create leaders.
Pragmatism is useful until it becomes a shield against uncomfortable questions.
Simplicity is not the absence of detail. It's the presence of clarity.
The greatest danger of intelligence is believing it makes us immune to error.
The purpose of a roadmap is alignment, not prediction.
